title: podman-container-runlabel(1): drop note
body:
Drop the note that Podman ensures to always be the first command.
Runlabels allows for executing any command on the host - which is
something we don't necessarily need to advertise or encourage.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

Just found that while preparing slides. @baude PTAL.

Eww .. there's another issue:
```
$ cat Dockerfile
FROM fedora:30
LABEL host touch /home/valentin/flock
$ podman build .
$ podman podman container runlabel host c8ef35edb4ed9b
$ command: podman /home/valentin/flock
```

Notice the stripped *touch* which was replaced by *podman*?
.. as it should be imho ... but i know we dont do that now. else lgtm
